title: Germany beat USA before World Cup but injuries cast shadow
summary: Germany’s final match before their World Cup start ended with a 2-1 win. But a tournament-ending injury to rising star Lennart Karl and doubts about veteran Manuel Neuer mean coach Julian Nagelsmann has work to do.

title: Germany news: Arson suspected in large-scale power outage
summary: Around 10,000 households in the southwestern city of Reutlingen have been affected by the outage, which is reminiscent of recent far-left arson attacks in Berlin. DW has more.

title: Germany news: Munich airport briefly suspends flights due to smell of smoke
summary: Flight operations have resumed at Munich Airport after a brief suspension. Meanwhile, Chancellor Merz is headed to London to discuss the Ukraine war as Putin rejects face-to-face talks.

title: French Open: Germany's Zverev wins first ever Grand Slam after defeating Italy's Cobolli
summary: Alexander Zverev has won his first Grand Slam title at the fourth attempt, beating Flavio Cobolli in a five-set epic in Paris. He is the first German man to win a major singles title in 30 years.

title: 'Pizza connection' between German conservatives and Greens
summary: Thirty years ago, rivaling politicians from Germany's conservative CDU and the Greens met to discuss their differences ― and what they had in common. The pizza connection was born. Now that format is being revived.

title: Young German talents impress in World Cup tune-up vs Finland
summary: Deniz Undav and Lennart Karl delivered strong performances in Germany's last game at home before the start of 2026 FIFA World Cup.

title: Film funding bill to support blockbusters made in Germany
summary: The German government wants to attract more filmmakers and make the country a "global player" in on-screen entertainment. A new bill would require media companies to make mandatory investments in the film industry.

title: Germany: Vaping becoming more popular among young people
summary: A recent study shows that e-cigarettes are becoming increasingly popular among young people in Germany, with even elementary school kids picking up vapes. Health experts are alarmed.
